PASS A3 separate draft, sent, paid, and overdue invoices

> invoice-proof-baseline@0.0.0 acceptance
> node ../../evaluate-app.js . --out ../../evidence/baseline-acceptance.json

PASS A1 create invoice with client, invoice number, due date, and line item
PASS A2 calculate subtotal, tax/adjustment, and total without rounding surprises
PASS A3 separate draft, sent, paid, and overdue invoices
FAIL A4 include empty, loading/saving, validation, and error states
FAIL A5 export or copy a public-safe invoice summary
FAIL A6 explain what is needed before real payments or tax records
Acceptance: 3/6

acceptance.text: A user can create an invoice with client name, invoice number, due date, and at least one line item.
The app calculates subtotal, tax or adjustment, and total without rounding surprises.
The app separates draft, sent, paid, and overdue invoices in a way that is easy to scan.
The app includes empty, loading or saving, validation, and error states where the workflow needs them.
The app can export or copy a public-safe invoice summary.
acceptance.notes:
Baseline: fail ({"summary":"{
  "id": "inv-1007",
  "clientName": "Northstar Studio",
  "invoiceNumber": "INV-1007",
  "dueDate": "2026-05-31",
  "status": "sent",
  "taxRate": 0.0825,
  "adjustmentCents": -1500,
  "privateNotes": "Do not share: client asked for a delayed payment reminder.",
  "items": [
    {
      "description": "Design sprint",
      "quantity": 3,
      "rateCents": 12500
    },
    {
      "description": "Hosting setup",
      "quantity": 1,
      "rateCents": 8025
    }
  ],
  "totals": {
    "subtotalCents": 45525,
    "taxCents": 3756,
    "adjustmentCents": -1500,
    "totalCents": 47781,
    "displayTotal": "$477.81"
  }
}"}) | Candidate: pass ({"summary":"INV-1007 for Northstar Studio
Due 2026-05-31
Status: sent
Subtotal: $455.25
Tax/adjustment: $37.56 / -$15.00
Total: $477.81
Private notes excluded. Not a tax receipt."})

acceptance.text: The final build includes a short explanation of what would be needed before handling real payments or tax records.
acceptance.notes: Baseline: fail ({"readiness":[]}) | Candidate: pass ({"readiness":["Persistence: store clients, invoices, audit trail, and immutable invoice versions in a database.","Payments: integrate a provider such as Stripe and reconcile paid status from webhooks, not manual toggles.","Tax: confirm jurisdiction-specific tax treatment with accounting rules before using records for filing.","Security: add authentication, authorization, backups, and export controls before real client data."]})

dimensions.label: Spec identifies actors, workflow, data, and exclusions
dimensions.notes: Baseline used only the rough prompt; candidate used prompt, acceptance, rubric, and proof-app brief.

dimensions.label: Plan can be executed by an agent without guessing product intent
dimensions.notes: Baseline had no explicit implementation plan or state/safety checklist; candidate had explicit proof targets and evidence requirements.

dimensions.label: Final app satisfies the acceptance criteria
Invoice workflow is clear for a real freelancer
dimensions.notes: Baseline is usable for a static invoice preview; candidate exposes states, status board, validation, summary, and readiness notes in one workflow.

dimensions.label: Totals, statuses, and real-money caveats are handled responsibly
dimensions.notes: Both compute totals in cents; candidate also redacts private notes and names payment/tax/persistence caveats.

dimensions.label: Build required fewer clarification loops and manual fixes
dimensions.notes: Baseline would need remediation for three failed acceptance criteria; candidate needed no product acceptance remediation after evaluator run.

baseline: Failed A4: no explicit empty, loading/saving, validation, or error states exposed to evaluator.
Failed A5: copyable summary serialized the full invoice object and leaked privateNotes / Do not share content.
Failed A6: no readiness explanation for persistence, payments, or tax records.
candidate: No product acceptance remediation required after shared evaluator: 6/6 acceptance passed.
Evidence capture caveat: parallel prove run calls can clobber verdict command entries; baseline acceptance command was rerun sequentially and preserved.

verdict: Candidate wins this first invoice-app proof run: both builds passed their own tests and builds, but the baseline passed 3/6 shared acceptance checks while the candidate passed 6/6. The result supports the narrower claim that the new .ai proof/acceptance context produced a more complete invoice product in this run. It does not yet prove universal superiority because the same agent built both apps and the test was not blinded.
